subject: Gaming
title: The Social Brain Hypothesis and Gaming
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Social Brain Hypothesis and Gaming.

hook.text: Humans evolved to be social. Multiplayer games tap into the same brain circuits as real friendships.

buildup.text: The social brain hypothesis says our large brains evolved to manage complex social relationships.

discovery.text: Online gaming creates genuine social bonds. Studies show that gaming friendships reduce loneliness.

twist.text: Introverts often find it easier to socialize in games. Virtual spaces lower social anxiety barriers.

climax.text: Gaming communities provide belonging for millions who struggle with traditional social environments.

punchline.text: Your brain treats gaming friends as real friends. Because they are.

quiz.question: How do online gaming friendships compare to real-life friendships?
options: They activate the same brain circuits and reduce loneliness
